title.raw: 《荊燕世家》 Houses of Jing and Yan

sentences.zh: 荊王劉賈者，諸劉，不知其何屬初起時。

translations.literal: King Jing Liu Jia was one of the various Liu clansmen; no one knows which branch he belonged to during the initial uprising.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Jia, the King of Jing, was a member of the Liu clan, though no one knows which branch he belonged to at the time of the initial uprising.

sentences.zh: 漢王元年，還定三秦，劉賈為將軍，定塞地，從東擊項籍。

translations.literal: In the first year of the Han King, after pacifying the Three Qins, Liu Jia served as general, secured the border regions, and followed east to attack Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the King of Han, after the Three Qin territories had been subdued, Liu Jia served as a general, pacifying the frontier regions before marching east to attack Xiang Ji.

translations.literal: King Jing Liu Jia was one of the various Liu clansmen; no one knows which branch he belonged to during the initial uprising. In the first year of Han King, after pacifying the Three Qins, Liu Jia served as a general, secured the border regions, and followed east to attack Xiang Yu.

sentences.zh: 漢四年，漢王之敗成皋，北渡河，得張耳、韓信軍，軍修武，深溝高壘，使劉賈將二萬人，騎數百，渡白馬津入楚地，燒其積聚，以破其業，無以給項王軍食。

translations.literal: In Han year 4, when the Han King was defeated at Chenggao and crossed north over the river, obtaining the armies of Zhang Er and Han Xin, he stationed at Xiuwu with deep moats and high ramparts, commanding Liu Jia to lead 20,000 men and several hundred cavalry across the Baima Ford into Chu territory, burning their grain stores to disrupt their livelihoods and prevent them from supplying Xiang Yu's army with food.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Han, after the King of Han suffered defeat at Chenggao and crossed the Yellow River northward, he joined forces with the armies of Zhang Er and Han Xin. He encamped at Xiuwu behind deep trenches and high ramparts, then dispatched Liu Jia with twenty thousand infantry and several hundred cavalry to cross at Baima Ford into Chu territory. There they burned the enemy's grain stores, devastating their supply lines so that Xiang Yu's army could no longer be fed.

sentences.zh: 已而楚兵擊劉賈，賈輒壁不肯與戰，而與彭越相保。

translations.literal: Soon after, Chu troops attacked Liu Jia, but Jia entrenched himself and refused to fight, instead holding out with Peng Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, Chu forces struck at Liu Jia, but he fortified his position and refused to engage, instead joining with Peng Yue to hold firm.

translations.literal: In Han year 4, when the Han King was defeated at Chenggao and crossed north over the river, obtaining the armies of Zhang Er and Han Xin, he stationed at Xiuwu with deep moats and high ramparts, commanding Liu Jia to lead 20,000 men and several hundred cavalry across the Baima Ford into Chu territory, burning their grain stores to disrupt their livelihoods and prevent them from supplying Xiang Yu's army with food. Soon after, Chu troops attacked Liu Jia, but Jia entrenched himself and refused battle, instead holding out with Peng Yue.

sentences.zh: 漢五年，漢王追項籍至固陵，使劉賈南渡淮圍壽春。

translations.literal: In Han year 5, the Han King pursued Xiang Yu to Gulang, commanding Liu Jia to cross south over the Huai and besiege Shouchun.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Han, the King of Han pursued Xiang Ji to Guling and ordered Liu Jia to cross the Huai River southward to lay siege to Shouchun.

sentences.zh: 還至，使人閒招楚大司馬周殷。

translations.literal: On the return, he sent agents to secretly recruit Chu's Grand Marshal Zhou Yin.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his return, he sent agents to covertly win over Zhou Yin, the Grand Marshal of Chu.

sentences.zh: 周殷反楚，佐劉賈舉九江，迎武王黥布兵，皆會垓下，共擊項籍。

translations.literal: Zhou Yin defected from Chu and assisted Liu Jia in capturing Jiujiang, welcoming King Wu Ying Bu's troops, all gathering at Gaixia to jointly attack Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Yin turned against Chu and helped Liu Jia seize the Jiujiang region. They then welcomed the forces of King Wu, Ying Bu, and all their armies converged at Gaixia to strike against Xiang Ji.

sentences.zh: 漢王因使劉賈將九江兵，與太尉盧綰西南擊臨江王共尉。

translations.literal: The Han King then commanded Liu Jia to lead the Jiujiang troops southwest with Grand Marshal Lu Wan to attack King Linjiang Gong Wei.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han then directed Liu Jia to lead the Jiujiang troops and, together with Grand Commandant Lu Wan, march southwest to attack Gong Wei, the King of Linjiang.

sentences.zh: 共尉已死，以臨江為南郡。

translations.literal: Gong Wei had already died, so Linjiang was made into Nanguo commandery.
translations.idiomatic: After Gong Wei's death, the territory of Linjiang was converted into the Nan Commandery.

translations.literal: In Han year 5, the Han King pursued Xiang Yu to Gulang, commanding Liu Jia to cross south over the Huai and besiege Shouchun. On the return, he sent agents to secretly recruit Chu's Grand Marshal Zhou Yin. Zhou Yin defected from Chu and assisted Liu Jia in capturing Jiujiang, welcoming King Wu Ying Bu's troops, all gathering at Gaixia to jointly attack Xiang Yu. The Han King then commanded Liu Jia to lead the Jiujiang troops southwest with Grand Marshal Lu Wan to attack King Linjiang Gong Wei. Gong Wei had already died, so Linjiang was made into Nanguo commandery.

sentences.zh: 漢六年春，會諸侯於陳，廢楚王信，囚之，分其地為二國。

translations.literal: In spring of Han year 6, he assembled the feudal lords at Chen, deposed King Chu Xin, imprisoned him, and divided his territory into two kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the sixth year of Han, the emperor assembled the feudal lords at Chen, deposed Han Xin, the King of Chu, had him imprisoned, and divided his territory into two kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 當是時也，高祖子幼，昆弟少，又不賢，欲王同姓以鎮天下，乃詔曰：「將軍劉賈有功，及擇子弟可以為王者。」

translations.literal: At this time, Gaozu's sons were young, his brothers few, and moreover not worthy, so he wished to enfeoff kings from the same surname to stabilize the realm. He issued an edict saying: 'General Liu Jia has merit, and I will select clansmen who can be made kings.'
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Emperor Gaozu's sons were still young, and his brothers were few in number and lacked distinction. Wishing to install kings who shared his surname to anchor the realm, he issued an edict: 'General Liu Jia has rendered great service. Let worthy members of the clan be selected and made kings.'

sentences.zh: 群臣皆曰：「立劉賈為荊王，王淮東五十二城；

translations.literal: All the ministers said: 'Establish Liu Jia as King of Jing, ruling over 52 cities east of the Huai;
translations.idiomatic: The assembled ministers all declared: 'Let Liu Jia be established as the King of Jing, to rule over fifty-two cities east of the Huai;

sentences.zh: 高祖弟交為楚王，王淮西三十六城。」

translations.literal: Gaozu's younger brother Jiao as King of Chu, ruling over 36 cities west of the Huai.'
translations.idiomatic: and let Jiao, the emperor's younger brother, be made King of Chu, ruling over thirty-six cities west of the Huai.'

sentences.zh: 因立子肥為齊王。

translations.literal: He then established his son Fei as King of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: He also established his son Fei as the King of Qi.

sentences.zh: 始王昆弟劉氏也。

translations.literal: This began the enfeoffment of brothers from the Liu clan.
translations.idiomatic: This marked the beginning of enfeoffing members of the Liu clan as kings.

translations.literal: In spring of Han year 6, he assembled the feudal lords at Chen, deposed King Chu Xin, imprisoned him, and divided his territory into two kingdoms. At this time, Gaozu's sons were young, his brothers few, and moreover not worthy, so he wished to enfeoff kings from the same surname to stabilize the realm. He issued an edict saying: 'General Liu Jia has merit, and I will select clansmen who can be made kings.' All the ministers said: 'Establish Liu Jia as King of Jing, ruling over 52 cities east of the Huai; Gaozu's younger brother Jiao as King of Chu, ruling over 36 cities west of the Huai.' He then established his son Fei as King of Qi. This began the enfeoffment of brothers from the Liu clan.

sentences.zh: 高祖十一年秋，淮南王黥布反，東擊荊。

translations.literal: In autumn of Gaozu year 11, King Huainan Ying Bu rebelled and attacked Jing to the east.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the eleventh year of Emperor Gaozu's reign, Ying Bu, the King of Huainan, rose in rebellion and attacked the kingdom of Jing to the east.

sentences.zh: 荊王賈與戰，不勝，走富陵，為布軍所殺。

translations.literal: King Jing Jia fought him but was defeated, fled to Fuling, and was killed by Bu's army.
translations.idiomatic: King Jia of Jing fought against him but could not prevail. He fled to Fuling, where he was slain by Ying Bu's forces.

sentences.zh: 高祖自擊破布。

translations.literal: Gaozu personally attacked and defeated Bu.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu personally led the campaign that crushed Ying Bu.

sentences.zh: 十二年，立沛侯劉濞為吳王，王故荊地。

translations.literal: In year 12, he established Marquis Pei Liu Pi as King of Wu, ruling over the former Jing territory.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year, he installed Liu Pi, the Marquis of Pei, as the King of Wu, to rule over the former Jing territory.

translations.literal: In autumn of Gaozu year 11, King Huainan Ying Bu rebelled and attacked Jing to the east. King Jing Jia fought him but was defeated, fled to Fuling, and was killed by Bu's army. Gaozu personally attacked and defeated Bu. In year 12, he established Marquis Pei Liu Pi as King of Wu, ruling over the former Jing territory.

sentences.zh: 燕王劉澤者，諸劉遠屬也。

translations.literal: King Yan Liu Ze was a distant relation of the various Liu clansmen.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ze, the King of Yan, was a distant kinsman of the Liu clan.

sentences.zh: 高帝三年，澤為郎中。

translations.literal: In Gaozu year 3, Ze served as a palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Emperor Gao's reign, Liu Ze served as a palace gentleman.

sentences.zh: 高帝十一年，澤以將軍擊陳豨，得王黃，為營陵侯。

translations.literal: In Gaozu year 11, Ze as a general attacked Chen Xi, captured Wang Huang, and was made Marquis of Yingling.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year, Liu Ze campaigned as a general against Chen Xi. He captured Wang Huang and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Yingling.

translations.literal: King Yan Liu Ze was a distant relation of the various Liu clansmen. In Gaozu year 3, Ze served as a palace attendant. In Gaozu year 11, Ze as a general attacked Chen Xi, captured Wang Huang, and was made Marquis of Yingling.

sentences.zh: 高后時，齊人田生游乏資，以畫干營陵侯澤。

translations.literal: During Empress Gao's time, a man from Qi named Tian Sheng, traveling without funds, sought patronage from Marquis Yingling Ze with his paintings.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Empress Dowager Gao, a man from Qi named Tian Sheng, who had exhausted his travel funds, approached the Marquis of Yingling with his stratagems.

sentences.zh: 澤大說之，用金二百斤為田生壽。

translations.literal: Ze was greatly pleased with him and used 200 jin of gold to celebrate Tian Sheng's birthday.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ze was greatly delighted with him and presented Tian Sheng with two hundred jin of gold as a gift.

sentences.zh: 田生已得金，即歸齊。

translations.literal: After Tian Sheng received the gold, he immediately returned to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Once Tian Sheng had the gold in hand, he promptly returned to Qi.

sentences.zh: 二年，澤使人謂田生曰：「弗與矣。」

translations.literal: Two years later, Ze sent someone to tell Tian Sheng: 'The matter is finished.'
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, Liu Ze sent a messenger to tell Tian Sheng: 'I will have nothing more to do with you.'

sentences.zh: 田生如長安，不見澤，而假大宅，令其子求事呂后所幸大謁者張子卿。

translations.literal: Tian Sheng went to Chang'an but did not see Ze. Instead, he rented a large residence and had his son seek service with Zhang Ziqing, the Grand Usher favored by Empress Lü.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Sheng traveled to Chang'an but did not call upon Liu Ze. Instead, he rented a grand residence and had his son seek a position under Zhang Ziqing, the Grand Usher who enjoyed the special favor of Empress Lü.

sentences.zh: 居數月，田生子請張卿臨，親修具。

translations.literal: After several months, Tian Sheng's son invited Zhang Qing to visit, preparing elaborate arrangements personally.
translations.idiomatic: After several months, Tian Sheng's son invited Zhang Qing to visit, personally preparing an elaborate feast for the occasion.

sentences.zh: 張卿許往。

translations.literal: Zhang Qing agreed to go.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qing accepted the invitation.

sentences.zh: 田生盛帷帳共具，譬如列侯。

translations.literal: Tian Sheng prepared lavish curtains and furnishings, comparable to those of marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Sheng laid out sumptuous curtains and furnishings rivaling those of a marquis's household.

sentences.zh: 張卿驚。

translations.literal: Zhang Qing was astonished.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qing was taken aback.

sentences.zh: 酒酣，乃屏人說張卿曰：「臣觀諸侯王邸弟百餘，皆高祖一切功臣。

translations.literal: When the wine flowed freely, he dismissed the attendants and spoke confidentially to Zhang Qing: 'I have observed that the sons and younger brothers of the feudal kings in the various mansions number over a hundred, all of them Gaozu's founding meritorious officials.
translations.idiomatic: When the wine was flowing freely, Tian Sheng dismissed the attendants and spoke to Zhang Qing in confidence: 'I have observed over a hundred sons and younger brothers of the feudal lords residing in the capital, all descendants of Emperor Gaozu's meritorious officials.

sentences.zh: 今呂氏雅故本推轂高帝就天下，功至大，又親戚太后之重。

translations.literal: Now the Lü clan, being old acquaintances, originally helped push Gaozu to obtain the realm, their merit is extremely great, and moreover they have the weighty kinship of being the empress dowager's relatives.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Lü clan, as old allies, originally helped propel Emperor Gao to conquer the realm. Their merit is immense, and they hold the additional weight of being close kinsmen of the Empress Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后春秋長，諸呂弱，太后欲立呂產為［呂］王，王代。

translations.literal: The empress dowager is advanced in years, the various Lü are weak, the empress dowager wishes to establish Lü Chan as king of Lü, ruling Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager is advanced in years, and the Lü are still weak. She wishes to establish Lü Chan as the King of Lü, to rule over Dai.

sentences.zh: 太后又重發之，恐大臣不聽。

translations.literal: The empress dowager repeatedly brings this up, fearing the ministers will not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the Empress Dowager hesitates to press the matter, fearing the ministers will refuse.

sentences.zh: 今卿最幸，大臣所敬，何不風大臣以聞太后，太后必喜。

translations.literal: Now you are most favored, respected by the ministers—why not sound out the ministers to inform the empress dowager? The empress dowager will certainly be pleased.
translations.idiomatic: You are the most favored at court and respected by the ministers. Why not hint to them to bring this matter before the Empress Dowager? She will certainly be delighted.

sentences.zh: 諸呂已王，萬戶侯亦卿之有。

translations.literal: Once the various Lü are kings, a marquisate of ten thousand households will also be yours.
translations.idiomatic: Once the Lü clan members are made kings, a marquisate of ten thousand households will be yours as well.

sentences.zh: 太后心欲之，而卿為內臣，不急發，恐禍及身矣。」

translations.literal: The empress dowager desires this in her heart, and you as an inner minister, if you do not act quickly, disaster may befall you.'
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager desires this in her heart. As an inner minister, if you do not act swiftly, I fear disaster will fall upon you.'

sentences.zh: 張卿大然之，乃風大臣語太后。

translations.literal: Zhang Qing greatly approved and began sounding out the ministers to speak to the empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qing wholeheartedly agreed and began dropping hints to the ministers, who then spoke to the Empress Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后朝，因問大臣。

translations.literal: At court, the empress dowager questioned the ministers.
translations.idiomatic: At the next court audience, the Empress Dowager raised the matter with the ministers.

sentences.zh: 大臣請立呂產為呂王。

translations.literal: The ministers requested establishing Lü Chan as King of Lü.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers petitioned to establish Lü Chan as the King of Lü.

sentences.zh: 太后賜張卿千斤金，張卿以其半與田生。

translations.literal: The empress dowager granted Zhang Qing a thousand jin of gold, and Zhang Qing gave half of it to Tian Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager rewarded Zhang Qing with a thousand jin of gold, and Zhang Qing gave half of it to Tian Sheng.

sentences.zh: 田生弗受，因說之曰：「呂產王也，諸大臣未大服。

translations.literal: Tian Sheng did not accept it, and instead advised him: 'Lü Chan is now king, but the various ministers are not yet greatly convinced.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Sheng declined the gift and counseled him: 'Although Lü Chan has been made king, the great ministers are not yet fully won over.

sentences.zh: 今營陵侯澤，諸劉，為大將軍，獨此尚觖望。

translations.literal: Now Marquis Yingling Ze, of the various Liu, serves as Grand General, and only he still feels disappointed.'
translations.idiomatic: At present, the Marquis of Yingling, Liu Ze of the Liu clan, serves as Grand General, and he alone still harbors resentment.

sentences.zh: 今卿言太后，列十餘縣王之，彼得王，喜去，諸呂王益固矣。」

translations.literal: If you speak to the empress dowager now and have her enfeoff him as king of more than ten counties, he will obtain kingship, leave happily, and the various Lü kingships will be even more secure.'
translations.idiomatic: If you now speak to the Empress Dowager and have her enfeoff him as king over ten or more counties, he will be delighted and depart the capital, making the Lü kingships all the more secure.'

sentences.zh: 張卿入言，太后然之。

translations.literal: Zhang Qing went in and spoke, and the empress dowager agreed to it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qing entered the palace and presented the proposal. The Empress Dowager approved.

sentences.zh: 乃以營陵侯劉澤為瑯邪王。

translations.literal: Thus Marquis Yingling Liu Ze was made King of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, Liu Ze, the Marquis of Yingling, was made the King of Langya.

sentences.zh: 瑯邪王乃與田生之國。

translations.literal: The King of Langye then went to his kingdom with Tian Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Langya then set out for his kingdom, taking Tian Sheng with him.

sentences.zh: 田生勸澤急行，毋留。

translations.literal: Tian Sheng urged Ze to proceed quickly without delay.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Sheng urged Liu Ze to travel with all haste and make no stops along the way.

sentences.zh: 出關，太后果使人追止之，已出，即還。

translations.literal: They passed through the passes, and the empress dowager indeed sent people to pursue and stop them, but they had already left, so they returned.
translations.idiomatic: Once they had passed through the frontier passes, the Empress Dowager did indeed send men in pursuit to stop them, but they had already left, and the pursuers returned empty-handed.

translations.literal: During Empress Gao's time, a man from Qi named Tian Sheng, traveling without funds, sought patronage from Marquis Yingling Ze with his paintings. Ze was greatly pleased with him and used 200 jin of gold to celebrate Tian Sheng's birthday. After Tian Sheng received the gold, he immediately returned to Qi. Two years later, Ze sent someone to tell Tian Sheng: 'The matter is finished.' Tian Sheng went to Chang'an but did not see Ze. Instead, he rented a large residence and had his son seek service with Zhang Ziqing, the Grand Usher favored by Empress Lü. After several months, Tian Sheng's son invited Zhang Qing to visit, preparing elaborate arrangements personally. Zhang Qing agreed to go. Tian Sheng prepared lavish curtains and furnishings, comparable to those of marquises. Zhang Qing was astonished. When the wine flowed freely, he dismissed the attendants and spoke confidentially to Zhang Qing: 'I have observed that the sons and younger brothers of the feudal kings in the various mansions number over a hundred, all of them Gaozu's founding meritorious officials. Now the Lü clan, being old acquaintances, originally helped push Gaozu to obtain the realm, their merit is extremely great, and moreover they have the weighty kinship of being the empress dowager's relatives. The empress dowager is advanced in years, the various Lü are weak, the empress dowager wishes to establish Lü Chan as king of Lü, ruling Dai. The empress dowager repeatedly brings this up, fearing the ministers will not listen. Now you are most favored, respected by the ministers—why not sound out the ministers to inform the empress dowager? The empress dowager will certainly be pleased. Once the various Lü are kings, a marquisate of ten thousand households will also be yours. The empress dowager desires this in her heart, and you as an inner minister, if you do not act quickly, disaster may befall you.' Zhang Qing greatly approved and began sounding out the ministers to speak to the empress dowager. At court, the empress dowager questioned the ministers. The ministers requested establishing Lü Chan as King of Lü. The empress dowager granted Zhang Qing a thousand jin of gold, and Zhang Qing gave half of it to Tian Sheng. Tian Sheng did not accept it, and instead advised him: 'Lü Chan is now king, but the various ministers are not yet greatly convinced. Now Marquis Yingling Ze, of the various Liu, serves as Grand General, and only he still feels disappointed. If you speak to the empress dowager now and have her enfeoff him as king of more than ten counties, he will obtain kingship, leave happily, and the various Lü kingships will be even more secure.' Zhang Qing went in and spoke, and the empress dowager agreed to it. Thus Marquis Yingling Liu Ze was made King of Langye. The King of Langye then went to his kingdom with Tian Sheng. Tian Sheng urged Ze to proceed quickly without delay. They passed through the passes, and the empress dowager indeed sent people to pursue and stop them, but they had already left, so they returned.

sentences.zh: 及太后崩，瑯邪王澤乃曰：「帝少，諸呂用事，劉氏孤弱。」

translations.literal: When the empress dowager passed away, King Langye Ze said: 'The emperor is young, the various Lü hold power, the Liu clan is isolated and weak.'
translations.idiomatic: When the Empress Dowager died, the King of Langya, Liu Ze, declared: 'The emperor is young. The Lü clan holds power, and the house of Liu is isolated and vulnerable.'

sentences.zh: 乃引兵與齊王合謀西，欲誅諸呂。

translations.literal: He then led troops west in conspiracy with the King of Qi, wishing to execute the various Lü.
translations.idiomatic: He raised his troops and conspired with the King of Qi to march west, intending to destroy the Lü clan.

sentences.zh: 至梁，聞漢遣灌將軍屯滎陽，澤還兵備西界，遂跳驅至長安。

translations.literal: When they reached Liang, hearing that Han had sent General Guan to garrison Xingyang, Ze returned his troops to defend the western border, then galloped swiftly to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached Liang, word came that the Han court had dispatched General Guan Ying to garrison Xingyang. Liu Ze sent his troops back to guard the western frontier and rode hard for Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 代王亦從代至。

translations.literal: The King of Dai also arrived from Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Dai had also arrived from his domain.

sentences.zh: 諸將相與瑯邪王共立代王為天子。

translations.literal: The various generals together with the King of Langye commonly established the King of Dai as Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The generals and ministers, together with the King of Langya, jointly enthroned the King of Dai as the new Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 天子乃徙澤為燕王，乃復以瑯邪予齊，復故地。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven then moved Ze to become King of Yan, and returned Langye to Qi, restoring the old territory.
translations.idiomatic: The new emperor then transferred Liu Ze to become the King of Yan and restored Langya to the kingdom of Qi, returning it to its former territory.

translations.literal: When the empress dowager passed away, King Langye Ze said: 'The emperor is young, the various Lü hold power, the Liu clan is isolated and weak.' He then led troops west in conspiracy with the King of Qi, wishing to execute the various Lü. When they reached Liang, hearing that Han had sent General Guan to garrison Xingyang, Ze returned his troops to defend the western border, then galloped swiftly to Chang'an. The King of Dai also arrived from Dai. The various generals together with the King of Langye commonly established the King of Dai as Son of Heaven. The Son of Heaven then moved Ze to become King of Yan, and returned Langye to Qi, restoring the old territory.

sentences.zh: 澤王燕二年，薨，謚為敬王。

translations.literal: Ze ruled Yan for two years, passed away, and was given the posthumous title King Jing.
translations.idiomatic: After ruling Yan for two years, Liu Ze passed away and was posthumously titled King Jing, the Reverent.

sentences.zh: 傳子嘉，為康王。

translations.literal: He passed the throne to his son Jia, who became King Kang.
translations.idiomatic: The throne passed to his son Jia, who became King Kang, the Tranquil.

translations.literal: Ze ruled Yan for two years, passed away, and was given the posthumous title King Jing. He passed the throne to his son Jia, who became King Kang.

sentences.zh: 至孫定國，與父康王姬姦，生子男一人。

translations.literal: Down to his grandson Dingguo, who committed adultery with his father King Kang's concubine, fathering one son.
translations.idiomatic: In the time of his grandson Dingguo, the prince committed adultery with his father King Kang's concubine, by whom he fathered a son.

sentences.zh: 奪弟妻為姬。

translations.literal: He seized his younger brother's wife as a concubine.
translations.idiomatic: He also seized his younger brother's wife and took her as a concubine.

sentences.zh: 與子女三人姦。

translations.literal: He committed adultery with three daughters.
translations.idiomatic: He also committed incest with three of his own daughters.

sentences.zh: 定國有所欲誅殺臣肥如令郢人，郢人等告定國，定國使謁者以他法劾捕格殺郢人以滅口。

translations.literal: Dingguo wished to execute a minister Fei, so he ordered the man from Ying to do it. The man from Ying and others informed on Dingguo, so Dingguo ordered the usher to indict and arrest them on other charges, executing them to silence them.
translations.idiomatic: Dingguo wished to have a certain official killed, the magistrate of Feiru, a man from Ying. When this man and others reported Dingguo's crimes, Dingguo had the court usher bring trumped-up charges against them, arresting and killing the man from Ying to silence him.

sentences.zh: 至元朔元年，郢人昆弟復上書具言定國陰事，以此發覺。

translations.literal: In Yuanshuo year 1, the man from Ying's brothers submitted another memorial fully detailing Dingguo's secret affairs, and thus it was discovered.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Yuanshuo era, the brothers of the man from Ying submitted another memorial laying out all of Dingguo's secret crimes, and the matter was at last brought to light.

sentences.zh: 詔下公卿，皆議曰：「定國禽獸行，亂人倫，逆天，當誅。」

translations.literal: The edict was issued to the ministers, and all discussed saying: 'Dingguo's conduct is that of beasts, disrupting human relationships, opposing heaven—he should be executed.'
translations.idiomatic: The case was referred to the high ministers, who all concluded: 'Dingguo's conduct is that of a beast. He has violated the bonds of human morality and defied the will of Heaven. He deserves execution.'

sentences.zh: 上許之。

translations.literal: The emperor approved it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor gave his assent.

sentences.zh: 定國自殺，國除為郡。

translations.literal: Dingguo committed suicide, and the state was abolished to become a commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Dingguo took his own life, and the kingdom was abolished and converted into a commandery.

translations.literal: Down to his grandson Dingguo, who committed adultery with his father King Kang's concubine, fathering one son. He seized his younger brother's wife as a concubine. He committed adultery with three daughters. Dingguo wished to execute a minister Fei, so he ordered the man from Ying to do it. The man from Ying and others informed on Dingguo, so Dingguo ordered the usher to indict and arrest them on other charges, executing them to silence them. In Yuanshuo year 1, the man from Ying's brothers submitted another memorial fully detailing Dingguo's secret affairs, and thus it was discovered. The edict was issued to the ministers, and all discussed saying: 'Dingguo's conduct is that of beasts, disrupting human relationships, opposing heaven—he should be executed.' The emperor approved it. Dingguo committed suicide, and the state was abolished to become a commandery.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：荊王王也，由漢初定，天下未集，故劉賈雖屬疏，然以策為王，填江淮之閒。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: King Jing became king because in the early Han, when the realm was not yet settled, thus although Liu Jia was distantly related, he was nevertheless made king through strategy, filling the region between the Jiang and Huai.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: The King of Jing owed his throne to the early days of the Han dynasty, when the realm had not yet been consolidated. Although Liu Jia was only distantly related to the imperial line, he was nonetheless made king as a matter of strategy, to anchor the region between the Yangtze and the Huai.

sentences.zh: 劉澤之王，權激呂氏，然劉澤卒南面稱孤者三世。

translations.literal: Liu Ze's kingship was provoked by the Lü clan's power, yet Liu Ze ultimately faced south and claimed isolation for three generations.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ze's kingship arose from a stratagem that exploited the power of the Lü clan, yet his line ultimately sat upon the throne, facing south as sovereign, for three generations.

sentences.zh: 事發相重，豈不為偉乎！

translations.literal: The events that occurred were significant—how can this not be considered remarkable!
translations.idiomatic: That events unfolded in such momentous succession — is this not truly remarkable!

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: King Jing became king because in the early Han, when the realm was not yet settled, thus although Liu Jia was distantly related, he was nevertheless made king through strategy, filling the region between the Jiang and Huai. Liu Ze's kingship was provoked by the Lü clan's power, yet Liu Ze ultimately faced south and claimed isolation for three generations. The events that occurred were significant—how can this not be considered remarkable!
